source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure CloudTrail configuration changes are monitored

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure CloudTrail configuration changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be used to detect changes to CloudTrail's configurations.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring changes to CloudTrail's configuration will help ensure sustained visibility into the activities performed in the AWS account. Impact: Ensuring that changes to CloudTrail configurations are monitored enhances security by maintaining the integrity of logging mechanisms. Automated monitoring can provide real-time alerts; however, it may require additional setup and resources to configure and manage these alerts effectively. These steps can be performed manually within a company's existing SIEM platform in cases where CloudTrail logs are monitored outside of the AWS monitoring tools in CloudWatch.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group- name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = CreateTrail) || ($.eventName = UpdateTrail) || ($.eventName = DeleteTrail) || ($.eventName = StartLogging) || ($.eventName = StopLogging) }" 4. Note the <cloudtrail-cfg-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <cloudtrail-cfg-changes- metric> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<cloudtrail-cfg-changes-metric>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for CloudTrail configuration changes and the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> -- filter-name <cloudtrail-cfg-changes-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=<cloudtrail-cfg-changes- metric>,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = CreateTrail) || ($.eventName = UpdateTrail) || ($.eventName = DeleteTrail) || ($.eventName = StartLogging) || ($.eventName = StopLogging) }' Note : You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note : Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <cloudtrail-cfg-changes- alarm> --metric-name <cloudtrail-cfg-changes-metric> --statistic Sum -- period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn> References: 1. CCE-79190-5 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored
input_row.impact: Ensuring that changes to CloudTrail configurations are monitored enhances security by maintaining the integrity of logging mechanisms. Automated monitoring can provide real-time alerts; however, it may require additional setup and resources to configure and manage these alerts effectively. These steps can be performed manually within a company's existing SIEM platform in cases where CloudTrail logs are monitored outside of the AWS monitoring tools in CloudWatch.
input_row.references: 1. CCE-79190-5 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control "Ensure CloudTrail configuration changes are monitored" can be fully verified via AWS APIs, boto3 SDK, and AWS CLI commands. The audit section provides detailed steps on how to use AWS CLI commands to describe CloudTrail trails, check CloudWatch Logs for metric filters, and verify CloudWatch Alarms and SNS subscriptions, indicating that these checks can be automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_trail_configuration_changes_monitored

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API calls / boto3 SDK methods to use:**
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()` to list all CloudTrail trails and identify multi-region trails.
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status(Name=<trail-name>)` to ensure the trail is active and logging.
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors(TrailName=<trail-name>)` to verify the trail captures all management events with `IncludeManagementEvents` set to true and `ReadWriteType` set to All.
  - `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=<log-group-name>)` to verify the presence of the metric filter with the specified filter pattern for CloudTrail configuration changes.
  - `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms(MetricName=<cloudtrail-cfg-changes-metric>)` to check that the CloudWatch alarm is associated with the correct metric.
  - `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn=<sns-topic-arn>)` to ensure there is at least one active SNS subscription.

- **AWS CLI commands (alternative):**
  - `aws cloudtrail describe-trails`
  - `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>`
  - `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>`
  - `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <log-group-name>`
  - `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<cloudtrail-cfg-changes-metric>]`
  - `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>`

- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - Enumerate all CloudTrail trails, CloudWatch Log groups, CloudWatch Alarms, and SNS topics in each AWS region.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - Ensure at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with logging enabled.
  - Verify that CloudTrail captures all management events.
  - Check for a specific metric filter pattern related to CloudTrail configuration changes.
  - Ensure a CloudWatch alarm is set up for the metric and that it triggers notifications via an SNS topic with active subscribers.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria:**
  - **Pass:** At least one multi-region CloudTrail trail is active and logging all management events, a metric filter is set up on the related CloudWatch Log group, a CloudWatch alarm is triggered by the metric, and an SNS topic has active subscribers to notify.
  - **Fail:** Any of the above conditions are not met.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively checked using AWS APIs, boto3 SDK, and AWS CLI commands. The described methods leverage AWS's capabilities to automate the verification of CloudTrail configuration changes monitoring.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The boto3 methods and CLI commands are valid for the tasks described. However, there are minor improvements that could enhance the assessment:
  - The `get_trail_status` method checks if the trail is logging but does not confirm if it is a multi-region trail. This should be verified using the `describe_trails` response.
  - The program name `aws_cloudtrail_trail_configuration_changes_monitored` is somewhat descriptive but could be more specific to indicate the monitoring aspect.
  - The use of `describe_metric_filters` and `describe_alarms` is appropriate, but the exact metric filter pattern should be specified to ensure it captures relevant CloudTrail configuration changes.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- **Program Name:** A more descriptive program name could be `aws_cloudtrail_monitor_configuration_changes`.
- **Metric Filter Pattern:** Specify the exact filter pattern to ensure it captures all relevant CloudTrail configuration changes. This might include patterns for `UpdateTrail`, `DeleteTrail`, `StartLogging`, and `StopLogging`.
- **Multi-Region Verification:** Ensure the `describe_trails` response is used to verify that at least one trail is configured as a multi-region trail.
- **AWS Config:** Consider using AWS Config rules to provide continuous compliance checks for CloudTrail configuration changes.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-structured and leverages AWS's capabilities effectively. The technical details, while mostly accurate, have minor areas for improvement, but overall, the approach is sound.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to be more descriptive, ensure the metric filter pattern is explicitly defined, and verify the multi-region configuration using the `describe_trails` response. Consider incorporating AWS Config for continuous monitoring.
